verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine that reaches the plasma membrane during secretion as part of the latent complex. In specific contexts—particularly on activated regulatory T cells and certain malignant B cells—it associates with cell surfaces via interaction with membrane-anchored partners (GARP/LRRC32 on Tregs, heparan sulfate on lymphoma cells) or molecular chaperones (GRP78). These surface associations are documented and functional, but depend on the protein's non-covalent interaction with other surface-resident molecules rather than TGFB1 possessing its own membrane anchor. The protein's baseline compartmentalization is the extracellular matrix (ECM) via LTBP-mediated anchorage, not the cell surface.
